text: Managing a team requires a blend of skills to keep everyone working well together. Clear communication is essential; you need to share goals and expectations so that team members know what to do. Effective delegation means assigning tasks based on people’s strengths, not just dumping work on them. Regular feedback helps the team improve—give both praise for good work and constructive suggestions for growth. To keep motivation high, recognize efforts and celebrate small wins. When conflicts arise, address them calmly by listening to both sides and finding a fair solution. By balancing these elements, a leader can create a productive and positive team environment.
